course.post_content:
Being adaptable means you are flexible, you can predict potential problems, you know how change affects you, and you respond in a way that allows for change without getting overly stressed.

 

I CAN

  1. Ican recognize when changes in the workplace offer me opportunities for growth.
  2. I can embrace new challenges at work in order to improve and expand my skill set.
  3. I can encourage others to share their ideas so that we work
    smarter toward our goals.
  4. I can take care of my mental wellbeing even when I make mistakes or get negative feedback.

_tutor_course_benefits:
Being adaptable means you are flexible, you can predict potential problems, you know how change affects you, and you respond in a way that allows for change without getting overly stressed.
Sees when things change and recognizes that change can be an opportunity.
Open to new experiences, including work environments, roles, and tasks.
Acknowledges the viewpoints of others and accepts suggestions to get the job done.
Handles average amounts of stress, positively uses feedback, and learns from things that go wrong.

children.post_content:
“Adaptability means the ability to be flexible and adjust to changing factors, conditions, or environments. Being adaptable is highly valued in almost all workplaces, since every industry and business can be unpredictable.” 

 

(Indeed.com)

Traits

  • Sees when things change and recognizes that change can be an opportunity.
  • Open to new experiences, including work environments, roles, and tasks.
  • Acknowledges the viewpoints of others and accepts suggestions to get the job done.
  • Handles average amounts of stress, positively uses feedback, and learns from things that go wrong.

Are Flexibility and Adaptability the Same?

Not exactly! Being flexible means you know a change has occurred and you may be required to change your behavior and attitude.

Being adaptable means:

  • you are flexible.
  • you can predict potential problems.
  • you know how change affects you.
  • you respond in a way that allows for change without getting overly stressed.

I Can Statements

  • I can recognize when changes in the workplace offer me opportunities for growth.
  • I can embrace new challenges at work in order to improve and expand my skill set.
  • I can encourage others to share their ideas so that we work together toward our goals.
  • I can take care of my mental wellbeing even when I make mistakes or get negative feedback.

Relevant Terms

FEEDBACK: a helpful response to someone, which is used for improvement 

GIG WORKER: Someone who works for a limited time period, as on a temporary, part-time, or project-based job

STRESS: The mental or emotional strain or tension that comes from experiencing a difficult situation

FIXED MINDSET: The belief that our abilities and intelligence are predetermined and do not change regardless of our efforts

GROWTH MINDSET: The belief that our abilities can change over time, as we experience and learn more

MULTI-DISCIPLINARY: combined or involving more than one discipline or field of study, or business and industry department

TRANSFERABLE SKILLS: skills you can take from one job or task to another

WORK/LIFE BALANCE: a healthy balance of work and personal commitments

Let’s start off our Adaptability training with looking at some common concepts and knowledge items surrounding adaptability and change. You’ll take a 10 question assessment to check your knowledge. Keep your score to see how it compares at the end.

answers.answer_title: A negative comment about someone\'s work
A helpful response to someone, which is used for improvement
Ignoring someone\'s efforts
Praise without any suggestions
Someone who works full-time for a single company
Someone who works part-time at multiple jobs simultaneously
Someone who works for a limited time period, as on a temporary, part-time, or project-based job
Someone who does not work at all
The feeling of being relaxed and calm
The mental or emotional strain or tension that comes from experiencing a difficult situation
The state of being happy and content
The feeling of excitement and anticipation

question.question_title: Which mindset believes that abilities and intelligence are predetermined and do not change?

answers.answer_title: Growth Mindset
Flexible Mindset
Fixed Mindset
Adaptable Mindset

question.question_title: What does a GROWTH MINDSET entail?

answers.answer_title: Believing that abilities are fixed and unchangeable
Believing that abilities can change over time, as we experience and learn more
Avoiding new challenges to stay comfortable
Ignoring feedback and improvement
Involving only one field of study or business department
Combined or involving more than one discipline or field of study, or business and industry department
Focusing only on technical skills
Avoiding collaboration between different fields
Skills specific to one job only
Skills you can take from one job or task to another
Skills that are not useful in any job
Skills that are only relevant in academic settings
Focusing solely on work and ignoring personal commitments
A healthy balance of work and personal commitments
Prioritizing personal life over work always
Working all the time without breaks

question.question_title: Describe a situation in which adaptability is important and explain why it is beneficial.

contents.post_content: Sees when things change and recognizes that change can be an opportunity

children.post_content:
When change occurs, how do I see it as an opportunity for personal and workplace growth?

  1. What life event caused a big change for Naomi?
  2. How did she embrace change as an opportunity?
  3. What skills did she already have from her prior residential mortgage finance job, or as a family caregiver, that helped her transfer to the new career at the hospital?

Transferable skills are those things you already know how to do which can be used in different situations. They are things you can take from one job to another.

When change happens we can embrace it by taking time to reflect on the transferable skills we have.  We are often just being asked to apply our skills in a different way.

Transferable Skills: 

What They Are and Why They Matter

Transferable skills are those things you already know how to do which can be used in different situations. They are things you can take from one job to another.

When change happens we can embrace it by taking time to reflect on the transferable skills we have.  We are often just being asked to apply our skills in a different way.

Download the handout and brainstorm some transferable skills.

Change is inevitable - how you deal with it is what matters and makes you stand out. Being adaptable and facing a challenge demonstrates to others that you want to grow.
Employers notice when you take on more work–and you position yourself in line for that next promotion!

Tell Your Story

  • Tell about how you approach unexpected challenges or changes in your work? Can you give an example of how you turned a challenge into an opportunity?
  • Can you tell about or describe a time when you faced a significant change in your school, job or life?  How did you adapt to this change, and what was the outcome?

Job Seekers Toolbox

  • Can you add “adaptable” to the skills on your Career Bio or resume’? Can you add any transferable skills? Add context on your resumé to let a prospective employer know you prepare for challenges in the workplace.
  • Tell about a transferable skill you used in a new situation.  What is the skill and how were you able to “transfer” it from one situation or job to another opportunity?

contents.post_content: Open to new experiences including work environments, roles, and tasks

children.post_content:
Essential Question

How will embracing challenges at work help me improve and expand
my skill set?

Start With a Positive Attitude

Being adaptable means you’re ready to change course if necessary.

Approaching each challenge with a positive attitude
allows you to adapt more quickly to reach a solution!

A negative attitude makes it more difficult to find a solution.

  1. Skill Development: Embracing challenges helps you develop new skills and improve existing ones, making you more versatile and capable in your job.
  2. Problem-Solving Abilities: Facing and overcoming challenges enhances your problem-solving abilities, enabling you to handle difficult situations more effectively.
  3. Career Growth: Taking on challenges can lead to new opportunities and responsibilities, contributing to your professional growth and career advancement.
  • Are you open to new ideas and methods?
  • Are you able to see setbacks or failures as an opportunity to learn and grow?
  • Can you step back and see the big picture?
  • Can you approach change with curiosity and creativity?

• Meta Fab Employees have to embrace change, new ideas, new roles, new experiences, and consistently embrace challenges.
  • Things are changing faster and faster.  The company has to anticipate and embrace change in machinery, roles, markets, and tasks. 
  • Let’s dive into some challenges that come with change, especially as it relates to awareness of effective listening and communication.

As you watch, think about: How do Meta Fab employees adapt to constant change and new challenges?  Why is listening and effective communication essential for success?

PROBLEM/SCENARIO:

MetaFab is considering a major investment in new equipment to stay competitive, causing excitement and unease among the team. The lack of communication from leadership and rumors of phasing out old equipment have led to concerns about job security and the fast pace of technological changes. The leadership needs to address these concerns, clarify the plan, and reassure the team during this transition.

Activity 

Scenario: You are working together as part of a Meta Fabs team to discuss the new piece of equipment the company is purchasing that will help increase production.  

Your small group of managers and technicians are meeting to communicate with employees the plan to purchase the new equipment.  The goal of the team meeting is to create a list of potential concerns and questions the employees might have, and how to reduce their fears and anxiety .  

Be sure to provide possible solutions that will help support all employees when the company announces the purchase of the new equipment.

Wrap-Up Questions:

  1. Why can change create anxiety and fear?
  2. How can a manager and team work to alleviate the challenges that come with change?
  3. What is the most important aspect of management for supporting people through a change in the workplace?

When we acknowledge our ability to learn, grow, and improve, we are more likely to:

  • reach our goals,
  • learn more advanced skills, and
  • be recognized for our valuable contributions.

Keyen (Keke) Singer: Co-Director NASU University of Oregon

  • How does Keyen’s role in NASU require her to understand and respect different perspectives and values when advocating for others?
  • Why does Keyen believe it’s okay to struggle with embracing your culture while adapting to a new environment, and how can this struggle help you grow?

NASU is the Native American Student Union is a multiethnic group that supports the educational goals of Indigenous Peoples, Alaskan Natives, and American Indians at the University of Oregon.

When we take on challenges at work, we open ourselves up to learning new skills. Being open to new experiences including work environments, roles, and tasks show you have a growth mindset.

Tell Your Story

  • Tell about an experience where you took on a challenging task or role that was outside your comfort zone? How did this experience help you develop new skills?
  • Tell how you stay motivated when faced with tasks or projects that require you to learn new skills or knowledge? Can you provide an example of how this attitude has benefited your work?

Job Seekers Toolbox

Three-Step Approach to Your Job Search:

  1. Make a list of skills you already have. Include technical and workplace skills. Also include any certifications you’ve received.
  2. Go online and research jobs/careers/opportunities that match the skills you have (see the Oregon CIS Job Search icon below). You are likely to find jobs you already knew about, but there may be some surprises, too.
  3. Based on your research, make a list of positions you want more information on.

contents.post_content: Acknowledges the viewpoints of others and accepts suggestions to get the job done.

children.post_content:
Essential Question

How will listening to my colleagues’ ideas create a positive and
successful workplace?

Listen for what skills are important when coming together to work on a project such as building drones. Why are skills such as understanding the importance of communication, understanding your knowledge & strengths and those of your team essential for success in innovative projects?
children.post_title: Industry Video: Umatilla Tribes Drone Program

children.post_content:
• University of Oregon brings together multi-disciplinary teams to solve real world problems.  
  • Listen to how many viewpoints they consider to get the job done.
  • See how many you can write down while you listen to the video.

You just created an “add on” for your gas tank that allows your car to go 100 miles on a gallon of gas. Brilliant! Now you have to assemble a multidisciplinary team to bring your product to market, and create a profit. Who do you need on your team? How will you find the team you need? Will you need to go outside your organization?

question.question_title: Make a list of team members you think you will need.

children.post_content:
Daniel works in accounting. He talks about the industry standard of working late nights during the busy season.  How did Daniel and his company work together to find an adaptable solution by first understanding each others’ viewpoints? 
What was the win-win that they came up with?

 

Navigate to Advocate

  • You just learned how Daniel navigated an alternate work schedule at KDP.
  • How can understanding our rights and responsibilities as students or employees help us navigate systems for support more effectively? 
  • What tools are in place for us to be able to understand our rights and responsibilities?
  • Why is persistence essential?  How can we adapt to being persistent as needed?

Share experiences in the following areas: 

  • Was there a time you can think of when knowing your rights and responsibilities enabled you to engage with support systems effectively?  
  • Was there a time when  a lack of understanding hindered your ability to access the support you needed?
  • Where can you find help, or who can you talk to, if you need help navigating a solution for your needs?

Summary

Asking for advice, getting help from others, and acknowledging that we don’t have all the answers enables us to face changes and adapt accordingly.

Tell Your Story

  • Tell how you typically approach asking for help when faced with a new or unfamiliar task? Can you share an example of how this approach has helped you successfully adapt to a change?
  • Tell about and describe a situation where you had to seek advice or assistance from others to overcome a challenge at work? What was the outcome, and how did it help you adapt to the situation?

Job Seeker's Toolbox

Cover Letter Checkpoint

Draft a brief description of how you demonstrate adaptability on the job. You can tailor this example to fit the specific jobs you apply for, but it will help to have the general description written.

Interview Checkpoint

  • Describe a time you relied on your coworkers’ suggestions in order to finish a job successfully.
  • Describe a time you learned a new skill because you were open to getting advice and assistance from a coworker.

contents.post_content: Handles average amounts of stress, positively uses feedback, and learns from things that go wrong.

children.post_content:
Essential Question

How can I protect my mental well being, even when I make mistakes on the job and receive negative feedback?

Stress occurs in our personal and workplace lives. Ignoring it doesn’t make it go away.

A growth mindset helps us be more adaptable.

  • We can keep negative emotions in check when we make mistakes.
  • We can change course and fix a problem.
  • We can see failure as an opportunity for growth.

Dealing with Stress

We all need a strategy for dealing with stress. 

  • Make a list of strategies you currently use to handle stress. 
  • Do an internet search to learn about other ways to reduce stress.
  • Practice stress-reducing techniques before you need them.

• How did Jessica embrace and handle the stress of having to step into her new role quickly, when the mentor left abruptly.
  • Jessica talks about how teaching is not a 9-5 job, but one that emotionally vests you in the work you do.  Why is it important to understand the physical and mental aspects of the job you plan to enter and if that is a good fit for your long-term well-being?

Interested in learning more? Watch Jessica’s Q&A

If you have “work/life balance,” you understand that along with hard work, you need to include things outside of work that you enjoy, and that help you refresh.  Too much work, or for that matter, too much fun, puts your time and energy out of balance and can affect your commitments and priorities.

Stress Less

Work/ life balance is essential for our mental well- being and our approach to life.  Stress can be a real part of life, but being aware of our stressors and how to effectively manage them is important.

Review the checklist on the right.  Add up the total of  your current stressors and
then answer the following questions:

  • What are some common stressors among us?
  • How do you usually cope with stress?
  • Are there any new strategies you'd like to try based on the items you marked on the checklist?

Where can we find additional support for stress if we need it?

Stressors Checklist: What’s Your #?

  • Academic pressure
  • Family conflicts
  • Peer pressure
  • Relationship issues
  • Financial concerns
  • Time management difficulties
  • Health problems
  • Future uncertainties
  • Social media overload
  • Personal insecurities
  • Lack of sleep
  • Performance anxiety
  • Balancing extracurricular activities
  • Bullying or harassment
  • Other

• Listen to how Erica used feedback to help her be successful in the workplace.  Why is listening to feedback from the audience you serve essential?
  • Erica talks about  how where she works, and her job environment are essential for work-life balance.  She enjoys being an educator working for a non-profit instead of working in a classroom.  Why do you think it’s important to understand work-life balance as a way to manage stress?

Interested in learning more? Watch Erica’s Q&A

Feedback, whether it is positive or negative, is about giving and receiving information that will help others perform better. Making a mistake or failing to complete a task correctly causes stress. Taking responsibility, rather than taking offense, reduces stress. Admitting that our plan may not work allows us to redirect our efforts to follow-up action.

question.question_description:
What went wrong with this feedback?

During a project review meeting, Hannah, the project manager, notices some errors in the work presented by Leopoldo, one of her team members. Instead of addressing the issues tactfully, Hannah bluntly criticizes Leopoldo’s work in front of the entire team, pointing out every mistake she found without offering any constructive feedback.

Leopoldo feels embarrassed and demoralized by Hannah’s approach. He becomes defensive and shuts down, unable to effectively absorb the feedback or participate in the discussion. The rest of the team feels uncomfortable witnessing the exchange, and the meeting becomes tense and unproductive.

What three strategies could have been done differently in this feedback scenario?

You are a shift supervisor at a retail store and the other shift supervisors you work with are complaining about the store manager to the new employees they are training.  How would you address the problem?

answers.answer_title: Ignore the problem it isn’t your problem.
Talk to the store manager and let them know what is going on, it isn’t professional and it’s causing issues with the store morale and employees are feeling uncomfortable.
Follow the store protocols of reporting issues like this to the district manager or the human resources team as outlined in the employee handbook.

children.post_content:
Inevitably, we will have problems that come up that we can’t solve by ourselves. It can be helpful to get an outside perspective. It is important to have a plan to deal with stress. A growth mindset leads us to success with less stress.

Tell Your Story

  • Tell about how you typically manage stress when faced with demanding tasks or tight deadlines? Can you share a specific strategy or plan that helps you stay focused and effective?
  • Tell about or describe a time when you made a mistake or failed to complete a task correctly? How did you take responsibility for the situation, and what steps did you take to reduce stress and address the issue?

Job Seeker’s Toolbox

Career Bio or Resume’ Checkpoint

Have you listed adaptability as a skill on your Career Bio or resume’? What evidence do you have?

Interview Checkpoint

  • Describe a time that you had to make a change. What role did listening to other viewpoints play in helping you adapt?
  • Describe a time when you had to adapt and change, which created stress. How did you handle the stress?
  • Describe a time when you were asked to do something you had never done before. How did you react? What did you learn?
  • Give an example of a time you demonstrated you had a growth mindset.

Project Based Learning: Industry Activities

Oregon Industry supported projects are a great way to dig in deeper on the concepts taught in OES.

Companies will provide resources, training & materials or offer various contests for students.

click here.
